text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off rotation out of megacap AI. VIX at 17.3 drifted sideways as hyperscalers sold hard while banks and semis rallied, leaving the S&P 500 and Nasdaq red against a green Dow and small caps. Mixed flows showed capital rotating from AI capex stories into financials and cyclical growth as Middle East supply hopes hit crude and AI infrastructure winners like Micron gained even as Alphabet dropped 10%.

INDICES — VIX 17.3, basically flat versus its 20-day at 17.4. SOXX +2.43% led, then IWM +0.88%, IJH +0.38%, DIA +0.30%, IJR +0.03%, RSP +0.00%, SPY -0.31%, QQQ -0.36%, with GLD -0.65% and SLV -1.01% lagging. STYLE: SMALL > LARGE by 1.19pp. Most notable cross-asset move was USO -1.90% on Middle East supply optimism while TLT -0.76% extended the modest backup in duration.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran below composite at roughly 0.9x while QQQ printed 1.1x, signaling higher attention and distribution in growth versus low-conviction selling in the broad market. DIA 1.4x and IWM 1.0x showed accumulation into cyclicals, while SOXX at 0.7x and RSP at 0.7x flagged that the semiconductor and equal-weight rallies came on lighter participation.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +0.54%, XLI Industrials +0.49%, XLV Healthcare +0.44%, XLK Technology +0.37%, XLRE Real Estate +0.36%, XLF Financials +0.24%, XLC Communications +0.00%, XLU Utilities +0.00%, XLB Materials -0.37%, XLP Staples -1.34%, XLY Discretionary -1.89%. DRAM +5.23% and XBI +3.65% topped industries while XHE -2.39% and SOCL -2.81% lagged. Money rotated into energy, industrials, and semis despite crude weakness, while consumer discretionary and staples were clear sources of funds as Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Palantir fell on AI capex worries. Overall tape reads as broadening participation with AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call at 0.79 with ATM IV 15.9% and positive skew 8.6% shows modest downside hedging but no panic as VIX sits effectively on its 20-day. QQQ put-call at 1.44 with skew 15.8% and VXN 27.7 above its 20-day 26.1 signal concentrated fear and protection demand in Nasdaq megacaps. IWM put-call at 1.98 with skew 11.3% and RVX 23.6 slightly below its 20-day 24.1 indicate hedged but not stressed small-cap risk, while MOVE at 70.0 below its 20-day 72.0 shows easing bond-vol stress.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength via UUP +0.21% alongside GLD -0.65% and SLV -1.01% highlighted classic risk rotation out of precious metals as rates nudged higher with TLT -0.76%. Oil’s USO -1.90% drop on Middle East supply progress contrasted with XLE +0.54%, underscoring equity investors buying energy balance sheets over the commodity.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 60.4 flashed Greed, with top movers including AGI -11.83% in gold miners, VRT under pressure despite a 103% six-month run in AI infrastructure, and FNGS -3.05% in high-beta tech baskets. SCHH volume at 6.0x with price +1.24% signals accumulation in REITs, while AGI 4.5x with price -11.83% marks clear distribution in gold. SpaceX’s 5% slide on a new bond sale and Alphabet’s 10% drop on AI capex concerns framed the day’s risk-off in hyperscalers. Watch EWH +1.53% on 2.9x volume for follow-through in Hong Kong, AIG +3.17% on 2.6x volume for sustained strength in insurance, and PBJ -0.78% on 3.1x volume for potential downside continuation in defensive consumer baskets.
